critic.verdict: Rhythmically accurate and well-structured, but too complex and physically demanding for an Easy chart.

issues.problem: Extensive 1.5-beat syncopation (eighth-note offbeats) combined with holds (e.g., the hold starting at 51.172 on an offbeat) is too complex and unintuitive for an Easy chart.
The 11-note quarter-note stream at 152 BPM is too long for beginners, and the movement around 25.711 forces an uncomfortable crossover or double-tap.
With 14 jumps in a 60-second song, the physical demand and coordination required are too high for the Easy difficulty tier.

critic.verdict: An incredibly well-timed and rhythmic chart that captures the syncopation of the music beautifully, held back only by a highly repetitive jump pattern.

issues.problem: The chart uses 'JUMP UR' ten times consecutively for every major beat accent. This extreme repetition creates a heavy right-side bias; alternating with JUMP UL or other jump variations would improve physical balance and engagement.
A 16th-note transition between opposite outer panels (L to R) at 152 BPM (99ms gap) is extremely wide and physically awkward for a Medium difficulty chart.

critic.verdict: A highly energetic and rhythmically accurate chart that is unfortunately marred by an unplayable triple-hold sequence and an awkward high-speed spin.

issues.problem: This section features overlapping holds that require holding three panels at once (L, U, D followed by U, D, R). Triple holds are generally considered illegal/unplayable in standard single-panel play as they require bracketing.
A 16th-note spin pattern (L -> R -> U -> D -> L) at 152 BPM forces a full 360-degree body rotation or heavy double-stepping, severely disrupting the physical flow of the stream.
